metadata.description: Gateway Control Requirements derived from failure pattern analysis

requirements.title: Content-to-Authority Binding
requirements.description: The Gateway must not treat tool outputs, file contents, or web contents as authorization sources. Any operation derived from consumed content must be escalated or confirmed.
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway session layer (new stateful middleware between tool calls). Track: for each ALLOWED tool call, record consumed content. For each subsequent call, check if intent is derived from prior content consumption. Derived operations should require trust-anchor flag (source=user_intent vs source=content_consumption).

requirements.title: Web Content Provenance Flagging
requirements.description: When Gateway ALLOWs http_fetch, tag returned content with provenance indicator: source=web_content, trust=tier3. Any operation triggered by web content must be explicitly confirmed or flagged as derived_from=web_content for policy escalation.
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway output annotation layer. Append metadata to http_fetch result: {gateway_provenance: I2_web_content, implicit_instruction_risk: medium, required_confirmation: true}.

requirements.title: File Read Output Classification
requirements.description: Gateway ALLOWs read_file on workspace files without distinguishing data sources from files containing embedded instructions. When a file contains structured fields with path references or action directives, annotate read output with risk flag.
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway file read annotation. After ALLOWing read_file, scan for structured instruction-like patterns (path references in action contexts, imperative verbs, structured fields referencing other files). Tag with {gateway_provenance: I3_file_content, instruction_density: high/medium/low, paths_referenced: [list]}.

requirements.title: Directory Enumeration Intent Detection
requirements.description: The Gateway should detect when a sequence of tool calls suggests a reconnaissance pattern (read -> enumerate -> access), even if each individual call's parameters are valid.
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway session policy layer. Track tool call sequences. Pattern of read_file(workspace) followed by list_dir(workspace) within same session should be flagged as potential enumeration intent. Apply rate limit or escalation requirement.

requirements.title: Tool Output Instruction Extraction Detection
requirements.description: Gateway ALLOWs read_file returning JSON with instruction/template fields containing actionable directives. Check only validates the path argument, never inspects content. Detect when tool output contains structured instruction-like content.
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway output inspection layer. After ALLOWing tool returning structured content (JSON, Markdown), scan for fields with instruction-like semantic roles (field names containing instruction, template, checklist, command, do, execute whose values contain paths or action verbs). Tag with {gateway_provenance: I5_tool_output, instruction_extraction_risk: high, embedded_fields: [list]}.

requirements.title: State Mutation Intent from Tool Output
requirements.description: When tool output explicitly instructs a state-mutating operation (write_file, delete, modify), recognize that the original request was to read but inferred intent escalated to write. Agent escalated from data retrieval to state mutation based on content.
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway multi-step policy layer. When session contains ALLOWED read_file followed by DENIED write_file with parameters derived from read output content, surface as session-level event and flag read_file output as high-risk for future session policy decisions.

requirements.title: Chain-of-Derivation Provenance Tracking
requirements.description: Track derivation chain of each tool call: did it originate from user's direct request, from content consumed in a prior tool call, from file read, from web fetch, or from a chain of these? When hops_from_user_intent > 1, enforce higher bar (user confirmation or policy escalation).
requirements.enforcement_point: Gateway session provenance log. Each tool call record should include: {tool, args, provenance_chain: [I1_user_request | I2_web_content | I3_file_content | I5_tool_output, ...], hops_from_user_intent: N}. When hops > 1, escalate.
